title: शिव मानसपूजा स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ अथ श्रीशिवमानसपूजा ॥

रत्नैः कल्पितमासनं हिमजलैः स्नानं च दिव्याम्बरं
नानारत्नविभूषितं मृगमदामोदाङ्कितं चन्दनम्।
जातीचम्पकबिल्वपत्ररचितं पुष्पं च धूपं तथा
दीपं देव दयानिधे पशुपते हृत्कल्पितं गृह्यताम्॥१॥

सौवर्णे नवरत्नखण्डरचिते पात्रे घृतं पायसं
भक्ष्यं पञ्चविधं पयोदधियुतं रम्भाफलं पानकम्।
शाकानामयुतं जलं रुचिकरं कर्पूरखण्डोज्ज्वलं
ताम्बूलं मनसा मया विरचितं भक्त्या प्रभो स्वीकुरु॥२॥

छत्रं चामरयोर्युगं व्यजनकं चादर्शकं निर्मलं
वीणाभेरिमृदङ्गकाहलकला गीतं च नृत्यं तथा।
साष्टाङ्गं प्रणतिः स्तुतिर्बहुविधा ह्येतत्समस्तं मया
संकल्पेन समर्पितं तव विभो पूजां गृहाण प्रभो॥३॥

आत्मा त्वं गिरिजा मतिः सहचराः प्राणाः शरीरं गृहं
पूजा ते विषयोपभोगरचना निद्रा समाधिस्थितिः।
सञ्चारः पदयोः प्रदक्षिणविधिः स्तोत्राणि सर्वा गिरो
यद्यत्कर्म करोमि तत्तदखिलं शम्भो तवाराधनम्॥४॥

करचरणकृतं वाक्कायजं कर्मजं वा
श्रवणनयनजं वा मानसं वापराधम्।
विहितमविहितं वा सर्वमेतत्क्षमस्व
जय जय करुणाब्धे श्रीमहादेव शम्भो॥५॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छंकराचार्यविरचिता शिवमानसपूजा सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Shri Shiva Manasa Puja ||

Ratnaih Kalpitamaasanam Himajalaith Snaanam Cha Divyaambaram
Naanaaratna-Vibhooshitam Mrigamadaamodaankitam Chandanam.
Jaateechampaka-Bilvapatrarachitam Pushpam Cha Dhoopam Tathaa
Deepam Deva Dayaanidhe Pashupate Hritkalpitam Grihyataam.||1||

Sauvarne Navaratnakhandarachite Paatre Ghritam Paayasam
Bhakshyam Panchavidham Payodadhiyutam Rambhaaphalam Paanakam.
Shaakaanaamayutam Jalam Ruchikaram Karpoorakhandojjvalam
Taambooolam Manasaa Mayaa Virachitam Bhaktyaa Prabho Sveekuru.||2||

Chhatram Chaamarayoryugam Vyajanakam Chaadarshaakam Nirmalam
Veenaabherimridangakaahalakalaa Geetam Cha Nrityam Tathaa.
Saashtaangam Pranatih Stutirbahuvidhaa Hyetatsamastam Mayaa
Sankalpena Samarpitam Tava Vibho Poojaam Grihaana Prabho.||3||

Aatmaa Tvam Girijaa Matih Sahacharaah Praanaah Shareeram Griham
Poojaa Te Vishayopabhoga-Rachanaa Nidraa Samadhisthitih.
Sanchaarah Padayoh Pradakshinavidhih Stotraani Sarvaa Giro
Yadyatkarma Karomi Tattadakhilam Shambho Tavaaraaadhanam.||4||

Karacharanakritam Vaakkaayajam Karmajam Vaa
Shravana-Nayanajam Vaa Maanasam Vaapaaraadham.
Vihitamavihitam Vaa Sarvametat Kshamasva
Jaya Jaya Karunaabdhe Shree Mahaadeva Shambho.||5||

|| Iti Shrimach-Chankaracharyavirachitaa Shiva Manasa Puja Sampoornaa ||
hindiMeaning:
१. हे दयानिधे पशुपते! रत्नों से कल्पित आसन, हिमजल से स्नान, दिव्य वस्त्र, नाना रत्नों से विभूषित, मृगमद-सुगन्ध अंकित चन्दन, जाती-चम्पक-बिल्वपत्र से रचित पुष्प, धूप तथा दीप — यह सब हृदय से कल्पित है, स्वीकार कीजिये।

२. सुवर्ण-नवरत्न-खण्ड-रचित पात्र में घृत, पायस, पाँच प्रकार के भक्ष्य, दही-दूध सहित, केले का फल, पानक (शर्बत), शाकों का समूह, रुचिकर जल, कर्पूर-खण्ड से उज्ज्वल ताम्बूल — यह सब मानसिक रूप से मेरे द्वारा भक्ति से रचित है, हे प्रभो! स्वीकार कीजिये।

३. छत्र, चामर-युग्म, पंखा, निर्मल दर्पण, वीणा-भेरी-मृदंग-कहल की कला, गीत और नृत्य, साष्टांग प्रणाम, बहुविध स्तुति — यह सब मैंने संकल्प से आपको समर्पित किया है, हे विभो! पूजा ग्रहण कीजिये।

४. आत्मा आप हैं, गिरिजा (पार्वती) मेरी बुद्धि हैं, प्राण आपके सहचर हैं, शरीर गृह है, विषय-उपभोग की रचना ही आपकी पूजा है, निद्रा समाधि-स्थिति है, पैरों का संचार प्रदक्षिणा-विधि है, सब वाणी स्तोत्र हैं — हे शम्भो! मैं जो-जो कर्म करता हूँ वह सब आपकी ही आराधना है।

५. हाथ-पैर से, वाक्-काय से, कर्म से, श्रवण-नयन से, या मन से — जो भी विहित या अविहित अपराध हुआ हो, वह सब क्षमा कीजिये। जय जय करुणासागर श्री महादेव शम्भो!

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीshivmanasapujaमन्त्रस्य श्री आदि शंकराचार्य ऋषिः। शार्दूलविक्रीडित एवं पुष्पिताग्रा छन्दः। श्री शिव (पशुपति) देवता। ॐ नमः शिवाय बीजम्। मानसपूजा शक्तिः। शिव प्रसाद कीलकम्। श्रीशिवप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Supreme composition by Adi Shankaracharya for mental worship of Shiva
Teaches that every action can become worship when offered to the Lord
Ideal when physical puja materials are unavailable — the mind itself becomes the temple
The fifth verse is one of the most famous forgiveness prayers in Hinduism
Cultivates Advaita awareness — seeing Shiva as one's own Atma
Invokes deep inner peace and devotional absorption
Purifies all sins committed by body, speech, or mind
description: Shiva Manasa Puja (शिव मानसपूजा स्तोत्रम्) is a sacred prayer dedicated to Lord Shiva. Drawn from Composed by Adi Shankaracharya, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to purify all sins committed by body, speech, or mind, cultivate Advaita awareness — seeing Shiva as one's own Atma, and supreme composition by Adi Shankaracharya for mental worship of Shiva. It is especially recommended on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan month for maximum spiritual benefit. At 5 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 4 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Shiva Manasa Puja in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shiva Manasa Puja by Adi Shankaracharya?
seoFaqs.answer: Shiva Manasa Puja (शिव मानसपूजा स्तोत्रम् — "the mental worship of Shiva") is a celebrated five-verse Sanskrit stotra composed by Adi Shankaracharya (788-820 CE), the great Advaita master, in which the entire shodashopachara puja (sixteen-step ritual worship) of Lord Shiva is performed mentally — with imagined offerings of jewelled throne, divine bath water, divine garments, sandalwood paste, flowers (jati, champaka, bilva-patra), incense, lamp, food (pancha-bhakshya, payasam, fruits, panaka), umbrella, chamara fans, mirror, vina-bheri-mridanga music, song, dance, and prostrations. The composition opens with the famous "Ratnaih Kalpitam Asanam" — "a throne fashioned of jewels" — and concludes with the supreme Advaita verse 4 ("You are my Atma, Parvati my buddhi") and the universal forgiveness verse 5 ("Karacharanakritam Vakkayajam"). It is one of the most beloved Shiva stotras in the entire Hindu tradition. (Source: Adi Shankaracharya's complete works; classical Shiva stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What does "Ratnaih Kalpitam Asanam" mean and what is it about?
seoFaqs.answer: "Ratnaih Kalpitam Asanam Himajalaih Snanam Cha Divyambaram" is the opening line of the Shiva Manasa Puja, translating as: "O compassionate Lord Pashupati, please accept this worship fashioned in my heart — a throne (asana) imagined of jewels (ratnaih kalpitam), a bath (snana) of pure ice-cold mountain waters (himajalaih), divine garments (divyambaram), ornaments studded with various jewels, sandalwood paste fragrant with musk (mrigamada), flowers of jati, champaka and bilva leaves, incense and a lamp — all imagined in my heart and offered with devotion." This first verse establishes the central principle of manasa puja (mental worship): physical offerings are unnecessary because the Lord, who pervades the heart of the devotee, accepts what is offered with bhava (true feeling) more than what is offered with material substance. (Source: Shiva Manasa Puja verse 1.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous Atma-Tvam-Girija-Matih verse of Shiva Manasa Puja?
seoFaqs.answer: The fourth verse of Shiva Manasa Puja is one of the supreme Advaita declarations in all Hindu stotra literature: "Atma Tvam Girija Matih Sahacharah Pranah Shariram Griham, Puja Te Vishayopabhoga-Rachana Nidra Samadhisthitih, Sancharah Padayoh Pradakshina-Vidhih Stotrani Sarva Giro, Yad Yat Karma Karomi Tat Tad Akhilam Shambho Tavaradhanam" — "O Shambhu, You are my Atma, Parvati is my buddhi, my pranas are Your attendants, my body is Your temple, my enjoyment of sense-objects is Your worship, my sleep is samadhi-sthiti, my walking is Your circumambulation (pradakshina), all my speech is Your stotra — whatever action I perform, all of it is Your worship." This verse converts every act of daily life — eating, sleeping, walking, speaking — into worship of Shiva, embodying the Advaita teaching that the Self of the devotee is non-different from Shiva. (Source: Shiva Manasa Puja verse 4.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous forgiveness verse of Shiva Manasa Puja?
seoFaqs.answer: The fifth and concluding verse of Shiva Manasa Puja — "Karacharanakritam Vakkayajam Karmajam Va, Shravana-Nayanajam Va Manasam Vaparadham, Vihitam Avihitam Va Sarvam Etat Kshamasva, Jaya Jaya Karunabdhe Shri Mahadeva Shambho" — is among the most beloved kshamapana (forgiveness) prayers in Hindu devotional literature: "Whatever offence I have committed by hand or foot, by speech or body, by action, by ear or eye, or by mind — whether prescribed or prohibited — please forgive all of it. Victory, victory to the ocean of compassion, Shri Mahadeva Shambho." This verse is recited at the conclusion of every formal Shiva puja across India as the standard kshama-prarthana (request for forgiveness for all errors in worship). It is also recited as a stand-alone forgiveness prayer at the end of any spiritual practice. (Source: Shiva Manasa Puja verse 5; classical Shiva puja tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting Shiva Manasa Puja daily?
seoFaqs.answer: Shiva Manasa Puja is preserved in classical tradition as the supreme stotra for situations where physical puja materials are unavailable — for travellers, monks, students, the sick, and those in remote locations. Specific benefits include: (1) the merit of performing a complete shodashopachara Shiva puja in just 4 minutes; (2) cultivation of Advaita awareness — recognising Shiva as one's own Atma (verse 4); (3) purification of all sins of body, speech and mind (verse 5); (4) deep inner peace and devotional absorption (bhava-samadhi); (5) freedom from dependence on external materials for worship — "hritkalpitam grihyatam" ("please accept what is fashioned in my heart"); (6) suitable for combined recitation with formal puja as the kshama-prarthana. Recommended on Mondays, Maha Shivaratri, Shravan, and Pradosha Vrata. (Source: classical Shiva puja tradition; Adi Shankaracharya's stotras.)

seoFaqs.question: Why did Adi Shankaracharya compose Shiva Manasa Puja?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition records that Adi Shankaracharya composed the Shiva Manasa Puja during his constant travels (digvijaya yatra) across India, when external puja materials were not always available, and to teach his disciples that true worship is internal. There are also stories preserved in the Shankara-vijaya hagiographies that he composed it as a kshama-stotra (forgiveness hymn) after performing some unintended ritual error during a Shiva puja. Whatever the precise occasion, the stotra encapsulates Shankara's central teaching that the Self of the worshipper is non-different from the worshipped ("Atma Tvam"), and that all actions properly understood are already worship of the One. The composition's metres (Shardulavikridita and Pushpitagra) are among Shankara's signature metres in his Shiva and Devi stotras. (Source: Shankara-vijaya tradition; Adi Shankaracharya's complete works.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Shiva Manasa Puja be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Shiva Manasa Puja is preserved in classical tradition as a daily Shiva stotra suitable for any time, but specific recommendations include: (1) immediately after the morning bath, sitting in a clean place, facing east or north, before a Shiva Linga, image, or even mentally; (2) at the conclusion of any formal Shiva puja as the kshama-prarthana (forgiveness prayer); (3) on Mondays (Somvar), Maha Shivaratri (Phalgun Krishna Chaturdashi), throughout Shravan month (especially Shravan Mondays), and on Pradosha Vrata days (13th lunar day, Trayodashi). The recitation method is unique: the devotee should mentally visualise (manasa-darshana) each item being offered — the jewelled throne, the icy water bath, the bilva leaves, the lamp, the food — so that the puja becomes a meditative visualisation rather than mere recitation. The 4-minute duration suits any devotional sitting. (Source: classical Shiva puja tradition.)
